description:
Goal
Make `target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry build fixtures2/00283_enum_reduce_while_shape.fz -o <out>` complete after semantic convergence. With fz-rh2.18.9.12 changes, the semantic drive test resolves, but AOT build now fails in native codegen: `native callable value for FnId(20) with 3 captures has no settled callable entry`.

Signal
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/timeout 60s target/debug/fz2 --log-telemetry <log> build fixtures2/00283_enum_reduce_while_shape.fz -o <out>` exits 0.
- The closed semantic executable frontier contains the callable entry required by the shape-changing reducer continuation, or the artifact planner proves it is not needed.
- The fix does not add backend shims, callable-entry adapters, retained-output workarounds, or fixture-specific Enum branches.

Strategy
Start from the logged failing frontier, identify whether semantic closure omitted a real latent executable or artifact lowering expects an executable that is not semantically live, and repair that boundary.

Outro
No missing callable entry at native lowering, no ad hoc AOT-only callable generation, no fixture-specific reducer workaround, and no weakening of semantic closure honesty.
title: Compiler2 Enum reduce_while AOT: close callable entry frontier after semantic convergence
